response.justification: Bogdan is a highly experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but the role's core requirement is deep, sustained C++ expertise — his C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with no evidence of STL-heavy, multi-threaded, or performance-critical C++ work since. His primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and domain experience (web, direct mail, geo-data) do not map to industrial automation, and there is no mention of IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any Secure Development Lifecycle practice that the role mandates.
strengths: Strong event-driven and distributed-systems background (microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP req/s, GISCollective Kubernetes platform) aligns with architectural patterns the role values.
Broad agile craftsmanship experience — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing — matches the behavioral and process expectations of the position.
Some exposure to IoT-adjacent work (geo-data collection platform, REST APIs, Node.js backend infrastructure) partially overlaps with the advantageous IoT skills listed.
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — the only substantive C/C++ work is a six-month stint at NI in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; no evidence of STL, multi-threading, or high-performance C++ in the last decade.
No industrial automation domain knowledge: zero mention of IEC 62443, SDL, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, or any industrial-cybersecurity context.
No Azure cloud experience and no Python scripting or automation work noted, removing two of the listed advantageous skills from consideration.
